bugs.root_cause: No early return/throw for edge case: isPending

bugs.error_message: Invalid code. Please check and try again — 400 on /api/auth/email-otp/verify-email despite correct OTP
bugs.root_cause: Used authClient.emailOtp.verifyEmail for sign-in OTP. That method is for type:email-verification only. Sign-in requires authClient.signIn.emailOtp({ email, otp }).
bugs.fix: Changed verifyEmail call to authClient.signIn.emailOtp in both enrolment.tsx and sign-in-form.tsx

bugs.error_message: Unauthenticated users land on onboarding screen instead of enrolment screen
bugs.root_cause: No auth guard — app had no logic to check session state and redirect unauthenticated users to /enrolment
bugs.fix: Added AuthGuard component using authClient.useSession() + useEffect redirect to /enrolment when no session

bugs.root_cause: Code path had no error handling — exceptions would propagate uncaught
title: "Enrol with TU/e", headerBackVisible: false → "Alumni Enrolment", headerBackVisible: false
bugs.fix: Changed title: "Enrol with TU/e", headerBackVisible: false → "Alumni Enrolment", headerBackVisible: false

bugs.error_message: Alumni OTP sign-in fails — no code works for alumni registry emails
bugs.root_cause: Email validation middleware was only applied to /api/auth/email-otp/send-verification-otp, not to /api/auth/sign-in/email-otp. The sign-in endpoint had no domain/registry check, meaning alumni emails were not consistently validated through the enrolment gate.
bugs.fix: Extracted validateEnrolmentEmail into a shared middleware function and applied it to both /send-verification-otp and /sign-in/email-otp paths.

bugs.root_cause: Async call without await — returned Promise instead of value
bugs.fix: Added await to async call | Also: app.post("/api/auth/sign-in/email-otp", async (c) => {; const res = await auth.handler(c.req.raw);

bugs.error_message: Wrong reference: true should be false

bugs.root_cause: Polar plugin had createCustomerOnSignUp: true. Polar's API validates email domains via DNS. alumni.tue.nl does not resolve as a valid DNS domain, so Polar rejects the email and Better-Auth returns 500.
bugs.fix: Set createCustomerOnSignUp: false — Polar customers are created lazily at checkout time rather than on every sign-up.

bugs.root_cause: Code path had no error handling — exceptions would propagate uncaught
bugs.fix: Added try/catch block | Also: createCustomerOnSignUp: false, // handled in databaseHooks w

bugs.root_cause: Code path had no error handling — exceptions would propagate uncaught

bugs.error_message: Alumni email not in registry shows student-centric error: 'Only TU/e institutional email addresses are accepted'
bugs.root_cause: isAllowedEnrolmentEmail() combined student+alumni validation into one boolean, so both rejection cases returned TUE_DOMAIN_ERROR regardless of whether the email was a failed alumni lookup. ALUMNI_REGISTRY_ERROR and ALUMNI_REGISTRY_UNAVAILABLE_ERROR constants existed but were unused.
bugs.fix: Split handleSendOtp into explicit branches: TU/e domain → proceed; non-TU/e + in alumni registry → proceed; non-TU/e + not in registry → ALUMNI_REGISTRY_ERROR (400); registry throws → ALUMNI_REGISTRY_UNAVAILABLE_ERROR (503). Removed isAllowedEnrolmentEmail helper.

bugs.root_cause: bun --hot only watches apps/server/src/ — changes to workspace packages (packages/api/) are not hot-reloaded. Server was running old compiled code that predated the new procedures.

bugs.error_message: nothing happens after accepting an invite
bugs.root_cause: acceptMutation.onSuccess only invalidated queries — no router.back() call, so user stayed on profile screen after accepting
bugs.fix: Added router.back() in acceptMutation.onSuccess, matching decline behavior

bugs.error_message: Encountered two children with the same key, `%s`. Keys should be unique... Spanish / English / Dutch / tech_coding / cosmology / etc.
bugs.root_cause: userLanguage and userInterest tables had no unique composite constraints (only index). onConflictDoNothing() never fired because id is a UUID (always unique). Running seed multiple times inserted duplicate rows. API returned arrays with duplicates causing React duplicate-key warnings.
bugs.fix: Added unique(userId, language, type) to userLanguage and unique(userId, interest) to userInterest. Manually deduped existing DB rows. Generated migration 0015_flat_mercury.sql.

bugs.fix: Changed pathname: "/partner/[id]", params: { id: req.requesterId, matchRequestId: req.matchRequestId → "/propose-meetup", params: { partnerId: match.partnerId, partnerName: match.partnerName
Rewrote 3→4 lines (3 removed) | Also: isDisabled={acceptMutation.isPending || acceptMuta; <Button.Label>{acceptMutation.isSuccess ? "Accepte

bugs.error_message: whenever i propose a meetup to a match nothing happens
bugs.root_cause: Two issues: (1) getMyMatches returned matches that already had a pending/confirmed meetup proposal, so proposing again silently conflicted or showed alert then immediately navigated back leaving match still visible; (2) router.back() fired immediately after Alert.alert() without waiting for user to dismiss, so user missed the success feedback
bugs.fix: (1) getMyMatches now filters out matches with active (pending/confirmed) proposals; (2) propose-meetup onSuccess moves router.back() inside Alert OK button callback; also invalidates getMyMatches cache
